mnemonics: MIPS (Measuring Important Performance Stats): Use MIPS to remember the core goal is to measure key performance indicators (like CPU, memory, disk I/O, network) to inform capacity decisions.
keyPoints: Measure performance against SLAs to ensure capacity meets business needs.
Focus on key resources: CPU utilization, memory consumption, disk I/O, and network bandwidth.
Establish a baseline and trend analysis to forecast future capacity requirements.
commonMistakes: Focusing on average utilization instead of peak demand, leading to under-provisioning.
Neglecting to correlate application performance with infrastructure metrics, missing the root cause of bottlenecks.
Failing to plan for future growth, resulting in reactive 'fire-fighting' instead of proactive scaling.
